title: practical english usage - Part 239
content:
As for that business of going to the police, I'm sorry I mentioned it.

As far as the kids are concerned, let's ask them what they think.

Regarding that car you were thinking of selling - how much  do you And speaking of Australia, did you see that documentary...?

7 saw Max and Emma  today. You know she - 'Talking of Max, you know as far as... is/are concerned; as for focusing: talking/speaking of/about; regarding; as regards; a link with previous discussion. Others show subdivisions and changes of topic.

Some discourse markers say what a speaker is going to focus on, and may show 1  What    are   we   talking     about?

the listener's expectations.

•    attitude They  can show a speaker's attitude to what he or she is saying, or to going on.

•    'What       are      we     doing?' They  can show the type of communication that is divisions and changes of topic.

•    'What       are           talking            about?         ’ we                          They  can introduce or clarify a topic, and show communicate several things: these (but not all) are also used in formal writing, ►    284. Discourse markers can speech. Those that are most common in speech are discussed here; some of kinds of discourse, some mostly in formal writing, and others mainly in informal case, to sum  up). English has a very large number of these. Some are used in all exchanges and written text. (e.g. first of all, by the way, on the other hand, in any Discourse markers are words and expressions which  help to structure spoken 301 discourse       markers      in  speech grammar •  301   discourse markers in speech you feel?

Look at it this way. Suppose you were in the same position as me. How would Why shouldn't she buy a horse if she wants to? After all, it's her money.

(►   305); negative questions (►  218) persuading: after all (►   361); look at it this way; look; question tags ‘ I don't like this stew.' ‘ Then/So don't eat it.' They didn't think I had enough experience, so I didn't get the Job.

showing              a   logical           or    causal           connection: so; then (►   583) of him.

I don 't dislike him  at all. On  the contrary / Quite the opposite, I'm very fond contradicting: on the contrary; quite the opposite The train was two hours late. In spite of that, we got to the match on time.

I'll do a lot for my family. However, there are limits.

He's 85. Yet he still takes care of the farm.

OK,  we've got to cut down expenses. But we can't get rid of the car.

She can be really irritating. On  the other hand, she's got a heart of gold.

marry one.

Bankers are generally nice people. Mind you, I wouldn't want my daughter to Jenkins wasn't on top form. Even so, he got two goals.

all the same.

I was a bit bored and lonely, yes, but I had a reasonably pleasant time needs redecorating. Still, it's home.

I'm glad to have a place of my own. OK,  it's a long way from the centre, and it on the other hand; but; yet; however; in spite of this/that making             contrasting                   points: still; all the same; even so; mind you; I'll eat anything apart from / except for chillies.

apart from; except for Broadly speaking, I get on pretty well with people.

To some extent, it's an interesting job. But there are some pretty dull patches.

'Do you think TV is bad for kids? ‘ Well, in some cases, maybe.' On  the whole / In general, people like to help others.

in all/most/many/some cases; broadly speaking generalising: on the whole; to some extent; in general; Some discourse markers make it clear what kind of communication is going on.

2 What    are   we   doing?

house. In a word, he's bone idle.

He gets up at midday. He won't look for a job. He doesn't help around the All in all, it wasn't the best trip we've had.

And the car broke down. And Jodie got mugged. And then Sophie was ill.
